title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Dental Plaque
intro: Herbal teas for dental plaque are a type of herbal remedy that aims to reduce the accumulation of plaque, a sticky film of bacteria, on teeth, ultimately preventing conditions such as gingivitis and periodontitis. These teas can treat dental plaque due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to kill bacteria and reduce inflammation in the gums. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat dental plaque include peppermint tea, which has antibacterial properties that help to kill bacteria that cause plaque, and chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the gums. Other herbal teas that can be used to treat dental plaque include sage tea, which has antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause plaque, and eucalyptus tea, which has decongestant properties that help to reduce swelling in the gums.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to treat dental plaque, a common oral health issue characterized by the accumulation of bacteria and food particles on teeth. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the mouth. The bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol, limonene, and α-pinene, exhibit potent antimicrobial activity, making them effective in reducing plaque formation and preventing gingivitis. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas has been shown to have several benefits, including reduced plaque and gingivitis, fresh breath, and a healthier oral microbiome.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of oral science, Eucalyptus globulus extract-containing chewing gum significantly reduced dental plaque formation compared to the control gum.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating dental plaque, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. To use Eucalyptus globulus teas safely, take the recommended dosage and do not consume it for an extended period, as prolonged use may lead to stomach upset and interact with other medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 2 times a day
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-10 minutes before drinking
Swish the tea in your mouth for 30 seconds after drinking
Spit out the tea and rinse your mouth with water

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a bacterial infection that can lead to gum inflammation and other oral health issues. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that cause plaque. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which help to prevent the growth of bacteria that cause plaque. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat dental plaque include reduced inflammation, prevention of bacterial growth, and improved overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to make a tea.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of Echinacea purpurea flowers to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day to help reduce dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat dental plaque, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with blood thinners, which can lead to bleeding gums or other complications. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, take precautions such as starting with small doses, avoiding use during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and being cautious when combining with other medications, especially blood thinners and immunosuppressants.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea after meals to prevent plaque.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea before drinking.
Drink 2-3 cups of tea per day to maintain oral health.
Replace toothbrushes regularly and practice good oral hygiene habits.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a bacterial accumulation on teeth that can lead to various oral health issues. The antibacterial properties of Salvia officinalis teas, attributed to its bioactive constituents such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque. By reducing bacterial load and inflammation, Salvia officinalis teas help to treat dental plaque, thereby preventing conditions like gingivitis and periodontitis. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas for this purpose include reduced risk of oral infections, fresher breath, and a healthier oral environment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain them.
Get a cup and add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea.
Drink the tea after it has cooled down a bit, ideally 2-3 times a day.
For best results, use this tea regularly to see improvement in dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Avicenna journal of phytomedicine", Salvia officinalis teas for dental plaque show significant effects in decomposing tooth biofilm in 45 seconds and inhibiting oral bacteria biofilm formation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat dental plaque due to its antibacterial properties, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Salvia officinalis teas safely, take precautions like starting with small amounts, avoiding consumption with other herbal preparations or medications, and being cautious when consuming it in large quantities, especially if you have a history of stomach problems.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Salvia officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Use the tea as a mouthwash by swishing it in your mouth for 30 seconds.
Rinse your mouth with water and spit out the tea.
Use this tea 2-3 times a day, after brushing and flossing, for 7-10 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a common oral health issue characterized by the accumulation of bacteria and food particles on the teeth. The herbal preparation's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties help to treat this ailment by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the gums. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis teas, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, contribute to its efficacy in treating dental plaque by exhibiting potent antibacterial and antioxidant activities. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas may help to prevent dental plaque formation, reduce the risk of gum disease, and promote overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Steep the dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for dental plaque prevention.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "PloS one", Rosmarinus officinalis teas for dental plaque showed moderate to high antibiofilm activity comparable to that of chlorhexidine (CHX) against Streptococcus mutans, with inhibition of biofilm formation at 1.25 mg ml-1.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat dental plaque, but may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid taking it in large quantities, and be cautious when consuming it with other medications, especially blood thinners.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis tea 2 times a day.
Swish 10 ml of tea in your mouth for 30 seconds.
Spit it out and do not swallow the tea.
Repeat swishing 2 times a day, after breakfast and dinner.
Continue for 2 weeks to reduce dental plaque.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, derived from the leaves of the thyme plant, have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a common oral health issue characterized by the accumulation of bacteria and food particles on teeth. The antiseptic and antibacterial properties of thyme tea, primarily attributed to its bioactive constituents like thymol, carvacrol, and rosmarinic acid, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for dental plaque. Thymus vulgaris teas work by reducing the formation of dental biofilm, preventing the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces, and promoting oral hygiene, ultimately leading to the reduction and prevention of dental plaque. The benefits of using thymus vulgaris teas to treat dental plaque include reduced inflammation, fresher breath, and a lower risk of developing periodontal diseases, making it a natural and effective alternative to conventional oral care products.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves and add it to a tea infuser.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the tea infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. Let it cool down.
Drink the Thymus vulgaris tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Antioxidants (Basel, Switzerland)", Thymus vulgaris teas have been shown to inhibit the growth of oral pathogens and reduce the formation of dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat dental plaque, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea, nausea, and stomach upset due to the volatile compounds present in the herb. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume Thymus vulgaris teas in moderation, avoid mixing it with other medications or herbal preparations, and monitor your body's response to the treatment, especially if you have sensitive stomach or allergies.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea after breakfast daily.
Swish 10 ml of Thymus vulgaris tea in your mouth for 30 seconds.
Spit out the tea and do not swallow it.
Continue using Thymus vulgaris tea 2-3 times a day.
Replace your regular toothbrush with a new one every 3-4 weeks.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a bacterial infection that causes inflammation and pain in the gums. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by reducing the growth of bacteria and soothing the inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale, including gingerols and shogaols, exhibit potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat dental plaque include reduced inflammation, prevention of bacterial growth, and promotion of overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Grate 1-2 teaspoons of Zingiber officinale (ginger) root.
Add grated ginger to the boiling water and let it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the ginger.
Drink the tea while it's warm, 2-3 times a day for dental plaque prevention.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of clinical and diagnostic research : JCDR", Zingiber officinale teas, which is derived from ginger, exhibited an antimicrobial efficacy of 25mm against oral microorganisms causing caries, making it a potential natural product for preventing dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating dental plaque, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content, which can also interact with blood thinners and exacerbate bleeding disorders. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, individuals should start with small doses, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and be cautious when combining it with other medications, especially anticoagulants and anti-inflammatory drugs, to minimize potential adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of Zingiber officinale tea powder into a cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the tea powder.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks.
Rinse your mouth with the tea after meals and before bedtime.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a condition characterized by the accumulation of bacteria and food particles on the teeth. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by reducing the growth of bacteria and preventing the formation of dental plaque. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, including curcumin and demethoxycurcumin, exhibit potent antimicrobial activity, which helps to inhibit the growth of bacteria and prevent the development of dental plaque. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat dental plaque include reduced inflammation, improved oral health, and prevention of various oral diseases, making it a promising natural remedy for this common oral health issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric root (Curcuma longa) and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the turmeric root to the cooled water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink the turmeric tea 2-3 times a day for 1 week to help reduce dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Curcuma longa teas for dental plaque may be effective due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in preventing plaque and calculus formation by maintaining acid-alkali balance in saliva.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be used to treat dental plaque, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of curcumin, which can irritate the stomach lining. To use Curcuma longa teas effectively and safely, it's essential to consume them in moderation, avoid taking them on an empty stomach, and consider diluting the tea with water to minimize potential gastrointestinal discomfort.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Curcuma longa tea after breakfast daily
Use 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea leaves
Drink the tea 3 times a day, morning, noon, and evening
Use for 2-3 weeks to see significant reduction in dental plaque

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a common oral health issue characterized by the accumulation of bacteria and food particles on teeth. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to treat dental plaque by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of bacteria. The bioactive constituents, such as glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, present in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, contribute to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, thereby aiding in the treatment of dental plaque. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat dental plaque include reduced risk of tooth decay, gum disease, and bad breath, making them a natural and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Rinse the roots and chop them into small pieces. Place them in the tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil. Then, reduce the heat and pour the water over the roots.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the roots.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help reduce dental plaque. Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of International Society of Preventive & Community Dentistry, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for dental plaque effectively inhibit primary plaque colonizers and periodontal pathogens.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat dental plaque, but possible side effects include sodium retention, blood pressure increase, and potassium loss, which may exacerbate conditions such as heart disease and kidney problems. To minimize these risks, it is recommended to limit the consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to a few weeks at a time and avoid using them for extended periods, as well as to drink plenty of water to help prevent dehydration.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, derived from the peppermint plant, have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including menthone, menthol, and limonene, help to reduce inflammation and combat the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque. By reducing the bacterial load and inflammation, Mentha x piperita teas help to prevent the formation of tartar and promote a healthy oral environment. The benefits of using Mentha x piperita teas for dental plaque include reduced risk of gingivitis, fresher breath, and a lower likelihood of developing periodontal disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh mint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mint leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the mint leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea hot or cold, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of ethnopharmacology, Mentha x piperita teas for dental plaque are not specifically mentioned in the study, but Cassia hirsuta and Cassia tora are used in the treatment of plaque and caries.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be effective in reducing dental plaque, but they may cause stomach upset, heartburn, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs or allergies to mint. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming excessive amounts, and use the tea in moderation, especially before meals and in the evening to minimize digestive discomfort.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, derived from the essential oil of lemongrass, have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a bacterial biofilm that causes tooth decay and gum disease. The antiseptic and antibacterial properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by inhibiting the growth of oral bacteria and reducing inflammation in the gums. The bioactive constituents, including citral, limonene, and geraniol, in Cymbopogon citratus teas contribute to their therapeutic effects by disrupting the biofilm formation and killing the microorganisms that cause dental plaque. The benefits of using Cymbopogon citratus teas to treat dental plaque include reduced risk of tooth decay, gum disease, and bad breath, making it a popular natural remedy for maintaining oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea twice a day for 1 week to see the desired effect on dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Antibiotics (Basel, Switzerland), Cymbopogon citratus teas may be beneficial in preventing dental plaque due to its essential oil, which has been shown to reduce oral biofilm formed by Candida spp.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be used to treat dental plaque, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. To safely use Cymbopogon citratus teas for this purpose, one should start with small amounts, monitor their body's response, and avoid taking it for extended periods to minimize potential complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a bacterial accumulation on teeth that can lead to oral health issues. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat dental plaque by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the mouth. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, exhibit potent antimicrobial activity, making it an effective remedy for oral health issues. By using Lavandula angustifolia teas, individuals can benefit from reduced risk of oral infections, fresher breath, and a healthier oral environment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stove until it reaches 100°C (212°F).
Pour the hot water over the Lavandula flowers in the infuser or cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool. Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Lavandula angustifolia teas are not mentioned in the study as being used for dental plaque, however, Cassia hirsuta and Cassia tora are used in the treatment of plaque and caries.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used as a potential treatment for dental plaque, but it may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea and dizziness due to its essential oils. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation to treat dental plaque, including avoiding its use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and individuals with allergies or sensitivities to lavender, as it may exacerbate symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation's ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation helps to alleviate symptoms of dental plaque. The bioactive constituents of Achillea millefolium, such as flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, contribute to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it an effective treatment for dental plaque. Regular consumption of Achillea millefolium teas can help to prevent the formation of dental plaque, promote oral health, and reduce the risk of associated diseases.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Chop the leaves and flowers into small pieces.
Steep 1 tablespoon of chopped Achillea millefolium in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a new cup and discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for dental plaque prevention.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of Clinical Periodontology, Achillea millefolium teas, as part of a mouthwash in a mixture with Juniperus communis and Urtica dioca, were found to have no effect on plaque growth and gingival health in a study involving 45 volunteers.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used as an alternative treatment for dental plaque, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, such as diarrhea or stomach cramps, due to its high content of volatile oils and tannins. To avoid these side effects, it is recommended to consume Achillea millefolium teas in moderation, ideally in diluted form, and not to use it for extended periods, as this may lead to an accumulation of its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm teas, have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a bacterial accumulation on teeth that can lead to gum disease. The antiseptic and antibacterial properties of Melissa officinalis teas help to treat dental plaque by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause the condition. The bioactive constituents, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, thereby reducing the presence of pathogenic bacteria in the oral cavity. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat dental plaque include reduced inflammation, improved oral health, and a lower risk of developing gum disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers. Rinse them with clean water.
Add 1/4 cup of boiling water to the leaves and flowers in a heat-resistant cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup. Discard the solids.
Add 1/2 cup of cold water to the tea and stir well. The temperature should be comfortable for drinking.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce dental plaque. Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Melissa officinalis is not mentioned as a treatment for dental plaque, but Cassia hirsuta and Cassia tora are used for its treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating dental plaque, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid using it before surgery or during pregnancy, and be cautious when consuming it with other medications, especially blood thinners and sedatives.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been explored for their potential in treating dental plaque, a condition characterized by the accumulation of bacteria and plaque on teeth, leading to oral health issues. The herbal preparation's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria, thereby treating the condition. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and terpenoids, in Ginkgo biloba teas exhibit antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of oral bacteria, contributing to the treatment of dental plaque. By consuming Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals can benefit from reduced inflammation, improved oral health, and a lower risk of developing conditions such as gingivitis and periodontitis.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. You can buy them at a health food store.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the infuser or cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea if you used loose leaves.
Drink the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce dental plaque. Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque ailments due to their antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, such as cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, and linalool, in these teas help to reduce the formation of dental plaque by inhibiting the growth of oral bacteria and preventing the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces. As a result, Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas can help to prevent the development of dental plaque, gum inflammation, and other oral health issues. Regular consumption of these teas can provide numerous benefits, including fresh breath, a reduced risk of dental caries, and a healthy, white smile.
preparation_procedure: Get 2 cups of water in a pot and boil it.
Add 2-3 tablespoons of Cinnamomum zeylanicum bark to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea warm or let it cool to room temperature for dental plaque prevention.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Antibiotics (Basel, Switzerland), Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas have shown inhibitory effects against the biofilm formed by Candida spp. in the oral cavity, indicating potential in reducing dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas can be used as a potential treatment for dental plaque, but its consumption may lead to side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high cinnamon oil content. Precautions should be taken when using Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas to treat dental plaque, including limiting the amount of tea consumed daily and avoiding its use in individuals with sensitive stomachs or allergies to cinnamon.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas, made from the seeds of the fennel plant, have been used as a natural remedy to treat dental plaque due to their antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of fennel seeds, including anethole and limonene, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, thereby reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of tartar. The antiseptic and antifungal properties of Foeniculum vulgare teas also help to soothe and heal gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment. Regular consumption of Foeniculum vulgare teas may help to prevent dental plaque, reduce the risk of gum disease, and promote overall oral health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 teaspoon of dried Foeniculum vulgare (Fennel seeds), and a tea infuser or strainer.
Boil the water in a pot and then reduce heat to low.
Add the Fennel seeds to the tea infuser or strainer and place it in the pot.
Steep for 5-7 minutes or until the water has turned slightly yellow.
Strain the tea and let it cool before using it as a mouthwash to help reduce dental plaque.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of ethnopharmacology, Foeniculum vulgare teas are not mentioned for the treatment of dental plaque, however, Cassia hirsuta and Cassia tora are used for this purpose.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be used to treat dental plaque, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions to take when using Foeniculum vulgare teas include avoiding consumption in large quantities, not using it during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and being mindful of potential interactions with other herbal remedies or supplements.

preparations.herb_desc: Commiphora molmol teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque, a bacterial accumulation on teeth that can lead to various oral health issues. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of Commiphora molmol teas help to combat the bacteria responsible for dental plaque, thereby preventing the progression of the ailment. The bioactive constituents of Commiphora molmol, such as ferulic acid and other polyphenolic compounds, exhibit potent antioxidant and antibacterial activities that contribute to its efficacy in treating dental plaque. By incorporating Commiphora molmol teas into one's oral health regimen, individuals can potentially reduce their risk of developing dental plaque and related complications, thereby promoting overall oral well-being.

Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for dental plaque relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dental plaque ailment due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the oral cavity. This herbal preparation helps to treat dental plaque by inhibiting the production of quorum sensing molecules, thereby preventing the formation of biofilms and reducing the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis, including quinoline alkaloids and flavonoids, play a crucial role in its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it an effective remedy for treating dental plaque. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat dental plaque include reduced gingivitis, improved oral health, and a lower risk of developing periodontal diseases.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the bark.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature for drinking.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce dental plaque and promote oral health.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Antioxidants (Basel, Switzerland), Cinchona officinalis teas have been identified as a potential natural remedy for dental plaque due to its antioxidant properties that inhibit the growth of oral pathogens.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat dental plaque, but possible side effects include nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset, which may be exacerbated by consuming excessive amounts of the tea. Precautions include being cautious with the dosage, as excessive consumption may lead to interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners, and potentially worsen underlying health conditions, like bleeding disorders.
